draft_v1: {"schema_v":1,"recipe":{"title":"THE WORLD'S MOST AWESOME STICKY BUNS","description":"NOTES : Being a night owl and a late riser, I like to prepare the buns and let them rise overnight in the fridge so all I have to do is bake them off in the morning. To do so, place the buns in the refrigerator immediately after covering them in step 5 and allow to rise for at least 6 hours, and up to 12. The next day, remove the buns from the fridge while the oven preheats, then proceed as directed.\nFor a nut-free version, the pecans can be omitted from the sauce.","yield_phrase":"MAKES 12 STICKY BUNS"},"steps":[{"instruction":"1. Make the dough : Whisk the eggs in a large bowl until homogeneous. Add the brown sugar, buttermilk, salt, yeast, and melted butter and whisk until homogeneous (the mixture may clump up a bit-this is OK). Add the flour and stir with a wooden spoon until a cohesive ball of dough forms.","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"3 large eggs"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1/3 cup packed light brown sugar"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"3/4 cup buttermilk"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 teaspoons kosher or 1 teaspoon table salt"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 teaspoons instant yeast"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"6 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"20 ounces (4 cups) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"4 tablespoons unsalted butter"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2/3 cup packed light brown sugar"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"3 tablespoons buttermilk"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"4 ounces (about 1 cup) toasted pecans coarsely chopped"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"Pinch of kosher salt"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2/3 cup packed light brown sugar"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 tablespoon ground cinnamon"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"4 ounces cream cheese"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1/4 cup buttermilk"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"11/2 cups confectioners' sugar"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 tablespoon grated orange zest (from 1 orange)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 tablespoons fresh orange juice"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"Pinch of kosher salt"}]},{"instruction":"2. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 2 minutes, or until completely homogeneous, smooth, and silky. Return to the bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and allow to rise at room temperature until roughly doubled in volume, about 2 hours.","ingredient_lines":[]},{"instruction":"3. Make the pecan-caramel sauce : Cook the butter and brown sugar in a small saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar is completely dissolved and the mixture is bubbling, about 2 minutes. Add the buttermilk, pecans, and salt and stir to combine, then pour the mixture evenly over the bottom of a 13-by-9-inch glass baking dish.","ingredient_lines":[]},{"instruction":"4. Make the filling : Combine the sugar and cinnamon in a small bowl and set aside.","ingredient_lines":[]}]}
warnings: Source description references a Step 5 while only 4 extracted_instructions are present.
